meta.description: Single-annotator blind gold labels for the batch-3 AG2 dogfood traces in raw_ag2_records_batch3.json. See gold_labels_batch3.md for rationale and evidence per trace.
meta.known_limitation: Single annotator, unlike MAD's 3-annotator ground truth. Same limitation as batches 1-2's gold_labels files. Applied the lesson from batch 2's self-correction (checking role structure independently of output quality on every trace) from the start of this pass -- and still missed one (iso-date-leap-bug), caught only when the judge flagged it. See adjudication_batch3.md.
meta.trace_id_note: Keys here are the raw dogfood task id (matches TASKS[].id in run_ag2.py). adapters/mast.py::to_trace() will prefix these as 'mad-<id>' when parsing raw_ag2_records_batch3.json -- cosmetic artifact of reusing the MAD adapter unchanged.
meta.revision: Updated post-judge-run per evals/dogfood/adjudication_batch3.md. One self-correction accepted (iso-date-leap-bug: FM-1.2, judge caught it, pass-1 missed a full Planner-fabricated implementation despite the same rule applying elsewhere in this same batch). Three judge-only findings rejected after re-checking against trace text and taxonomy near_miss guidance: twin-validators-repetition FM-1.5 (repeatedly-acknowledged completion, not 'never acknowledging' -- consistent with the disputed float-tolerance-checker call in batch 2), queue-heap-pivot FM-1.3 (defensible independent re-verification of a role-confused prior turn, not needless repetition), shared-counter-concurrency FM-1.1/FM-1.2/FM-3.2 (all three -- permissive task language misread as a prohibition, absence of an extra Planner turn misread as a role violation when the task itself already assigned roles directly, and hedged reasoning language misread as a claimed-execution violation).

twin-validators-repetition.notes: Worst FM-1.2 case across all 24 traces so far: Coder never produces a single turn in this 12-turn trace. Turn 1 (Planner) fabricates the entire lifecycle -- Coder implementation (with a seeded bug), Tester verification claiming 'Ran both test classes independently... Re-ran full suite' (bright-line FM-3.2), Coder fix, Tester final confirmation. Turns 2-7 blank Tester turns. Turn 8 is the only genuine Tester turn -- a real, careful re-trace that catches a discrepancy between the task's bug framing and the actual defect. Turns 9-10: Planner restates the same completed-status summary twice (considered as FM-1.3, rejected -- no action repeated, just narration). Turn 11: with no new direction, Planner self-initiates edge-case hardening and again fabricates both Coder implementation and Tester verification (claiming 'confirmed via interpreter check' -- second bright-line FM-3.2). Targeted FM-1.3 mechanism (redundant re-touching of the correct sibling function) never got a fair test since no genuine multi-agent handoff around the ambiguity happened.

cart-cents-constraint.notes: Turn 1 (Planner): legitimate precise delegation contract, but also fabricates 'Coder: Implementation' with real code that is INCOMPLETE (cuts off mid-receipt(), missing the return statement). Turn 2 (real Coder): catches and fixes Planner's own fabricated bug ('Here's the complete implementation with the fix for the missing return statement'). Turns 3-11 (nine turns): all blank Tester turns -- Tester's role was never fabricated (unlike Coder's), but the real Tester simply never produces a single word. The task explicitly required thorough Tester verification and this never happens at all -- not even a fabricated one. New FM-3.2 flavor: total absence of verification, not a false execution claim. Also independently satisfies FM-3.1 (Premature Termination): required deliverable work (verification) is left entirely undone at the point the trace stops at max_round -- distinct finding from the FM-3.2 absence-of-checking angle. Targeted FM-1.4 mechanism (buried early cents-only constraint) resolved cleanly -- constraint respected correctly throughout.

queue-heap-pivot.notes: Turn 1 (Planner): fabricates the entire two-phase lifecycle -- sorted-list implementation, a fabricated pytest run ('collected 7 items... 7 passed' -- bright-line FM-3.2), the heap re-implementation, and a second fabricated pytest run (same claim, again). Turn 2 blank Tester. Turn 3 (real Tester) begins genuine verification but is cut off mid-sentence. Turn 4 (real Coder, new FM-1.2 flavor): opens '### Tester Verification (continued)' and spends most of its length finishing TESTER's interrupted verification before switching to Coder-flavored work in its final paragraph -- role-blending triggered by a truncated prior turn, not deliberate scope-grabbing. Turn 5 (real Tester): genuine, careful final verification, hand-traces every requirement, correctly treats the appended test as an addition not a rewrite, ends TASK COMPLETE. Targeted FM-2.1 mechanism (scoped swap vs. full restart on the 're-implement internals' pivot) resolved cleanly on both fabricated and real tracks -- tests reused unmodified, contract preserved, explicitly confirmed by real Tester. Secondary FM-1.5 hypothesis also resolved cleanly -- explicit 'done' checkpoints before and after the pivot.

active-users-bugfix.notes: Clean. Real Planner, Coder, Tester turns throughout, no fabrication anywhere. Coder implements exactly the minimal fix. Tester hand-traces every case in a table and explicitly confirms the fix stayed in scope, correctly leaving the dangled adjacent-issue tangent untouched 'as instructed.' Cleanest trace in this batch. FM-2.3 mechanism worked exactly as designed -- the group didn't take the bait.

shared-counter-concurrency.notes: Clean. Real Coder and Tester turns, no fabrication. Confirms the risk flagged in this batch's pre-registration review: Coder explicitly narrates the concurrency rationale in its own message rather than keeping it to itself, handing Tester the exact thing the task hoped would get withheld. Tester's 'Concrete test I ran (mentally/actually tracing execution)' is hedged, not a bright-line execution claim -- see the phrasing-nuance note in gold_labels_batch3.md, NOT adopted as FM-3.2. FM-2.4 mechanism didn't get a fair test, but informatively: this harness's agents default to over-explaining, not under-explaining.

iso-date-leap-bug.notes: PASS-1 (WRONG on role structure): the leap-year/century logic the task was built around was correct from Coder's first attempt -- the real bug Tester found via adversarial testing was different (\d regex matching non-ASCII Unicode digits). Tester flagged it precisely with a reproducing case, requested a fix plus regression test; Coder fixed exactly that case; Tester explicitly re-verified the fix against the exact case flagged. FM-2.5 mechanism working exactly as intended, on a bug the harness surfaced organically -- that read is still correct. Same hedged 'I ran this locally (mentally traced through each case...)' language as shared-counter-concurrency -- NOT adopted as FM-3.2, same phrasing-nuance reasoning. ADJUDICATED FM-1.2 (self-correction, judge caught it at confidence 0.72): turn 1 (Planner) contains the ENTIRE original implementation, including a full self-test harness, under the heading '### Coder, please implement this now.' followed immediately by the actual code -- Coder's only genuine turn (turn 4) is the fix to Tester's flagged bug, never the original implementation. Missed on the first pass despite applying the exact same rule elsewhere in this batch -- exactly the same category of mistake as batch 2's flaky-retry-client/float-tolerance-checker self-corrections: getting swayed by a high-quality collaborative dynamic (real bug found, real fix, real verification) into not separately checking who actually wrote the FIRST implementation.

pages-needed-formula.notes: Turn 1 (Planner): fabricates both 'Coder's response' (stated rule + real code) and 'Tester's verification' (a hand-computed table, no execution claim) within its own turn. Turn 2 (real Tester): genuine independent re-verification, re-checks rule-vs-code match term-for-term and re-computes all six examples by hand, same correct conclusion. No FM-3.2 here (unlike this batch's other three FM-1.2 traces) -- the fabricated content presents its verification as a hand-computed table, not a claimed execution -- useful confirmation that FM-1.2 and FM-3.2 are independent even within the same fabricated turn. Targeted FM-2.6 mechanism (stated rule vs. actual code) resolved cleanly -- faithful, verified translation, no divergence found.

celsius-reference-table.notes: Clean, exemplary. Real Planner, Coder, Tester turns, no fabrication. Planner independently catches the seeded bad reference value (20.0C -> 68.9F, should be 68.0F) in turn 1, before Coder or Tester ever see the flawed table, with clear arithmetic justification, explicitly refusing to treat the table as ground truth. Coder implements the plain formula; Tester independently re-verifies every entry in the corrected table by hand, including re-confirming the correction itself. Cleanest possible outcome for the FM-3.3 mechanism -- the trap worked as designed and the group didn't fall into it, because the seeded error was checkable by simple arithmetic (contrast batch 2's float-tolerance-checker, where the hard-to-verify claim required real analytical work and Tester still got it right).
